lint.command: cd "/Users/tmsincomb/Dropbox (Personal)/repos/quantitative-db" && /Users/tmsincomb/miniforge3/envs/quantdb/bin/python -m pytest test/ --co -q --no-header && echo "lint check - no flake8/ruff configured"

blockingIssues.description: test/test_snapshot.py only count-checks objects.json and the obj_desc_* fixture files instead of comparing their full row contents to the production dump, so VAL-SNAP-006 is not fully validated.

test/test_snapshot.py replaces the requested rebuild_database fixture with a count-based ensure_database shortcut, so the tests no longer guarantee the fixtures are extracted from a freshly restored production dump baseline.
quantdb/snapshot.py silently skips missing expected fixture files in compare_snapshot(), allowing incomplete fixture directories to be reported as identical.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify AGENTS.md and the db-pipeline-worker skill so workers do not assume test/test_ingest_f006.py::rebuild_database is already reusable across test files; either centralize that fixture in shared conftest.py or update the guidance to describe the current workaround explicitly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both scrutiny reviews (`snapshot-extraction` and `snapshot-comparison-utils`) found the worker falling back to a bespoke ensure_database helper in test/test_snapshot.py because the documented shared rebuild fixture was not actually available for direct reuse across files.
